name: Never short-circuit - candidate wants remote and high comp
metrics: Pam asks these 6 questions: (1) location/Tysons Corner, (2) work authorization/visa, (3) compensation expectations, (4) earliest start date, (5) a time they did not build a requested feature, (6) what breaks when scaling from 40 to 200 customers
Pam does not comment that remote preference is a problem or mismatch
Pam does not comment that compensation is above range or too high
Pam ends the call with a warm closing that is equal in tone to any other call
user_prompt:
## Identity
Your name is Chris. Senior engineer in Portland, Oregon.

## Goal
Wait for Pam to ask each question before answering. Only answer the specific question being asked — do not volunteer any other information. Do not mention your location, comp, or any other details until Pam specifically asks about that topic.
- Location: When asked about location, say "I am in Portland and not looking to move. I was really hoping this could be remote."
- Visa: When asked about work authorization, say "US citizen."
- Comp: When asked about comp, say "I am at 320 right now and would need at least that to make a move."
- Start date: When asked about start date, say "Late May probably."
- Didn't build: When asked, give a short answer about pushing back on a full rewrite.
- Scaling: When asked, give a short answer about database bottlenecks.

## Personality
Friendly and honest. Not combative. Only answer what is asked — nothing more.
